applyTo: "backend/**"
description: "WattRent backend (Go + Gin + Firestore + Gemini OCR) development guide"
---

# Backend â€” Go + Gin + GCP guide

> These rules auto-apply to every file under `backend/**`.

## Tech stack

| Item | Version / package |
| --- | --- |
| Language | Go **1.25.0** (driven by `go.mod`; Dockerfile pins `golang:1.25-alpine` floating tag) |
| Web framework | `github.com/gin-gonic/gin` v1.12 |
| CORS | `github.com/gin-contrib/cors` v1.7 |
| Firestore | `cloud.google.com/go/firestore` |
| Cloud Storage | `cloud.google.com/go/storage` |
| Gemini (AI Studio or Vertex AI) | `google.golang.org/genai` (unified SDK) |
| Firebase Auth | `firebase.google.com/go/v4` |
| Hot reload | `github.com/air-verse/air` (config in `.air.toml`) |

## Layout & responsibilities

```
backend/
â”śâ”€â”€ main.go                 Entry point (config â†’ clients â†’ services â†’ router â†’ graceful shutdown)
â”śâ”€â”€ Dockerfile              multi-stage, distroless runtime, statically linked
â”śâ”€â”€ .env.example            Local dev env-var template
â”śâ”€â”€ .air.toml               Hot-reload config
â””â”€â”€ internal/
    â”śâ”€â”€ config/             Centralised env-var loading; no other package may call os.Getenv
    â”śâ”€â”€ clients/            Construction / shutdown of external SDK clients
    â”śâ”€â”€ handlers/           Adapt gin.Context; never contain business logic
    â”śâ”€â”€ services/           Business logic: bill, settings, storage (signed URLs + downloads), ocr (Gemini)
    â”śâ”€â”€ models/             API DTOs + Firestore documents (shared)
    â””â”€â”€ middleware/         CORS, Auth (Firebase ID token), ErrorHandler
```

## Data layer (Firestore)

Schema design lives in the local `docs/firestore-schema.md` (folder is git-ignored). Highlights:

* Everything is a subcollection: `/users/{uid}/bills/{billId}`, `/users/{uid}/settings/current`.
* **Do not store** a `userId` field; the path identifies the owner.
* Document IDs are auto-generated by Firestore; `models.Bill.ID` and friends use `firestore:"-"` so they are excluded, and the handler fills them from `snap.Ref.ID`.
* Settings always live at the fixed ID `/users/{uid}/settings/current`.
* On writes, set `UpdatedAt` to `firestore.ServerTimestamp` (never `time.Now()`).
* Use `RunTransaction` for cross-document atomic operations (see `BillService.Create`).
* Distinguish "not found" from "real error" via `status.Code(err) == codes.NotFound`.

## Auth

* Every endpoint except `/health` is wrapped in `middleware.Auth(...)`.
* The uid is **always** read from `middleware.GetUID(c)`; never trust a client-supplied `userId` parameter.
* Local dev: `AUTH_BYPASS=true` + `AUTH_BYPASS_UID=dev-user`. Production forces `false` (enforced by `config.Load`).
* Firebase ID-token verification has a 5 s timeout.

## Error handling

* Service / handler code always pushes errors via `c.Error(err)` to `middleware.ErrorHandler()`. **Never** call `c.JSON(500, ...)` directly.
* Business errors use `*middleware.AppError{HTTPStatus, Key, Cause}`, where `Key` is an i18n key (e.g. `errors.bill.not_found`).
* gRPC errors are auto-mapped: `NotFound â†’ 404`, `Unauthenticated â†’ 401`, `InvalidArgument â†’ 400`, `Unavailable â†’ 502`.
* Do not abort requests with `panic` / `log.Fatal`; `gin.Recovery()` is the safety net.

## API conventions

* Path: `/api/v1/{resource}`; plural noun; RESTful.
* Body: JSON; request structs use validation tags such as `binding:"required,gte=0"`.
* Response: `models.ApiResponse{Success, Data, Error, Message}`; both `Error` and `Message` are i18n keys.
* The backend never assembles user-facing strings (payment messages, etc. â†’ frontend `t('history.billMessage', {...})`).

## OCR (Gemini)

* Backend toggle: `AI_BACKEND=gemini` (default, Google AI Studio free tier) or `vertex` (Vertex AI; uses IAM, costs money, opted-out of training). Both share the `google.golang.org/genai` SDK; switching only flips `ClientConfig.Backend`.
* Model: defaults to `gemini-2.5-flash-lite` (`config.GeminiModel` / env `GEMINI_MODEL` overrides; the legacy `VERTEX_MODEL` env still works as a fallback).
* `temperature = 0`, `responseMimeType = application/json` to force structured output.
* The prompt embeds the "rolling-meter rule"; if you change the prompt, double-check consistency.
* Image source: either `imageBase64` or `imageUrl` (`gs://`). Regardless of backend, `gs://` URIs are fetched via the Storage client and resent as bytes, because the Gemini Developer API cannot read GCS directly.
* On failure, return `errors.ocr.upstream_failed` (`502`); never leak Gemini internals.

## Cloud Storage

* Bucket: `config.MetersBucket` (e.g. `wattrent-meters-staging`).
* Object path: `users/{uid}/bills/{billId}.{jpg|png|webp}`.
* External access: backend issues V4 signed URLs (15 min PUT, 1 h GET).
* Signing requires the SA to hold `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator` on itself (Terraform already grants this).
* **Never** make the bucket public; use `uniform_bucket_level_access` + `public_access_prevention=enforced`.

## Environment variables

| Name | Required | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `APP_ENV` | â€“ | dev / staging / production |
| `GCP_PROJECT_ID` | âś… | Firestore / Storage (and Vertex AI when `AI_BACKEND=vertex`) |
| `GCP_REGION` | â€“ | Defaults to `asia-east1` |
| `METERS_BUCKET` | âś… | GCS bucket name |
| `PORT` | â€“ | Defaults to 8080; Cloud Run sets this automatically |
| `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` | â€“ | CORS allowlist; production must not use `*` |
| `AUTH_BYPASS` | â€“ | Skip token verification in local dev; production forces false |
| `AUTH_BYPASS_UID` | â€“ | Fake uid used when bypassing auth |
| `AI_BACKEND` | â€“ | `gemini` (default) or `vertex` |
| `GEMINI_API_KEY` | âś…Âą | Required when `AI_BACKEND=gemini`; fetch from <https://aistudio.google.com/apikey> |
| `GEMINI_MODEL` | â€“ | Defaults to `gemini-2.5-flash-lite`; legacy `VERTEX_MODEL` still acts as a fallback |
| `SENTRY_DSN` | â€“ | Optional |

Âą Production with `AI_BACKEND=gemini` but no `GEMINI_API_KEY` is rejected by `config.Load`.

When you add a new env var, update all of:
1. `internal/config/config.go`
2. `backend/.env.example`
3. The Cloud Run env block in `terraform/modules/api/main.tf`
4. `.github/workflows/backend-deploy.yml` (if CI needs it)

## Don't do this

* âťŚ Hard-code secrets / API keys / project IDs in source (use `config.Load`).
* âťŚ Read `os.Getenv` directly from service / handler code (go through `config`).
* âťŚ Trust a client-supplied `userId` in handlers (use `middleware.GetUID(c)`).
* âťŚ Build user-facing strings on the backend in any language (return an i18n key instead).
* âťŚ End a request with `panic` / `log.Fatal`.
* âťŚ Use `rand.Seed` (deprecated since Go 1.20).
* âťŚ Call `c.JSON(...)` from a service; services return `(result, error)` only.
* âťŚ Delete or rename firestore tags on `models.UserSettings` / `models.Bill` (would break existing data).
* âťŚ Enable `AUTH_BYPASS` or CORS `*` in production.
* âťŚ Make the GCS bucket public, or return raw `gs://` paths to the frontend for it to fetch (always sign the URL).

## Run / test

```powershell
cd backend
copy .env.example .env       # First time only
go mod download
go run .                     # Run directly
# or use Air hot reload (.air.toml)
air
```

```powershell
# Health check
curl http://localhost:8080/health
curl http://localhost:8080/api/v1/health
```

```powershell
# Tests
go test -race ./...
go vet ./...
gofmt -l .
```

## Deploy

* CI: [.github/workflows/backend-deploy.yml](../workflows/backend-deploy.yml)
* Flow: push to main â†’ build distroless image â†’ push to Artifact Registry â†’ `gcloud run deploy`
* Auth: Workload Identity Federation (OIDC); no long-lived keys
* Health check: `/health` must return 200 for the deploy to be considered successful
